rationale: Top-3 US seller every year; high-dollar repairs where the dealer-vs-DIY gap is largest.
#2 best-selling vehicle in America; enormous parc, high repair spend.
Best-selling non-truck in the US; the highest-parc SUV segment overall.
Perennial top compact SUV; 1.5T oil-dilution TSB history makes the verified-fitment reveal strong.
Top-5 seller; the Jatco CVT complaint pattern is a textbook 'is this quote fair' fraud-shield moment.
High-volume compact SUV; EcoBoost coolant-intrusion TSBs are a high-value, high-anxiety search.
Adds Mazda coverage; loyal, research-heavy owner base that shops repair prices.
Very high on-road population; 2.4L timing-chain/oil-consumption issues are prime fraud-shield territory.
Top midsize/3-row SUV; high repair spend and a large, price-sensitive family-owner base.
Complex, high-dollar repairs (air suspension, electronics) where dealer quotes are steepest.
Massive, engaged owner base and heavy TSB volume; strong DIY-and-mods crossover.
Top-selling midsize sedan; CVT failure pattern is one of the most-searched 'am I being ripped off' repairs.
Theta II 2.4L engine recall/knock-sensor saga: high volume, safety-flagged, and a powerful verified-vs-dealer story.
Fiercely loyal owner base; documented oil-consumption/head-gasket issues that owners actively research.
Adds Kia coverage; shares the Theta II engine-recall angle with a family-hauler, budget-conscious ICP.
Silverado's mechanical twin under a separate NHTSA nameplate; large, distinct owner base at low incremental ingestion cost given near-identical TSB corpus.
Best-selling mid-size pickup; zero mid-size-truck coverage in the KB so far, and a huge DIY-owner audience.
Top-selling minivan; zero minivan coverage in the KB, and minivans skew toward exactly RAPP's price-sensitive family-owner ICP.
Subaru's actual best-seller (bigger parc than Outback); shares the same head-gasket/oil-consumption narrative already justified for Outback.
High-volume mainstream sedan; extends GM coverage beyond trucks/SUVs.
High-volume mid-size sedan; extends Ford coverage beyond Escape/Explorer/F-150 with well-documented transmission/recall history.
Adds a non-Big-3/non-mainstream-Asian brand; popular compact sedan with a documented DSG/timing-chain known-issue narrative.
Extends Hyundai coverage beyond Sonata; very high-volume compact sedan.
Closes a real gap: zero hybrid-powertrain vehicle in the KB despite the app's own vehicle schema supporting a 'Hybrid' powertrain field. Best-selling hybrid ever made.
High-volume compact sedan; extends Nissan coverage beyond Rogue/Altima's CVT-focused narrative.
Distinct nameplate from Grand Cherokee -- compact-SUV segment with a well-documented 9-speed transmission complaint history, strong fraud-shield narrative fit.
Extends Kia coverage beyond Sorento; popular midsize sedan.
3-row family-hauler segment gap; extends GM coverage beyond trucks/compact SUVs/sedans.
Notorious DPS6 dry-dual-clutch transmission issues with real class-action history -- one of the strongest 'you're not imagining it, here's the verified defect' narratives available.
Toyota-brand minivan; complements Odyssey for cross-brand minivan-segment coverage.
